channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: G2 Esports Didn’t Scrim Fnatic for the Entirety of LEC Spring
item.content:encoded:
G2 Esports did not scrim rivals Fnatic once throughout the LEC Spring Split 2026 on their road to the title.

G2 won their third LEC split in a row, defeating Karmine Corp 3-2 in yet another thrilling five-game series between the two teams.

The League of Legends team’s general manager, Romain Bigeard, revealed G2’s scrim results throughout the split, which revealed some surprising details.

One of which was that G2 did not play any practice games against their oldest rivals, Fnatic.

Fnatic Are in Trouble

Fnatic are continuing to struggle this year, having failed to even qualify for the Playoffs altogether in Spring.

It’s possible that G2 didn’t think Fnatic would give them enough competition in practice.

G2 also didn’t scrim against Team Heretics, Shifters, or SK Gaming, the other three teams that failed to make the Playoffs.

However, G2 did play against tier-two teams Galions and Solary, who impressed against LEC opposition in the Esports World Cup Qualifiers.

In their current form, Fnatic now find themselves at risk of missing out on the LoL World Championships for the first time in 10 years.

Fnatic have attended every LoL Worlds from 2017 onwards and attended the majority of the events before that too.

The team is also in a long trophy drought, having not won a title since Summer 2019.

As a result, Fnatic are apparently looking to replace their toplaner with a Korean pro to try and turn their season around.

G2 Scrims Anger KCorp Fans

Fnatic fans weren’t the only ones upset with G2’s scrimming decisions.

The post from Bigeard revealed that G2 stopped scrimming Karmine Corp four weeks into the split.

This was most likely so that they had an edge over their main title contenders and didn’t make their opposition stronger.

Some KC fans were furious over this, claiming that G2 were holding their region back by not allowing other teams to get stronger.

Do you agree with G2’s decision to dodge KC scrims or is it cowardly? Share your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord. 

G2 have also secured their future results by extending Steven ‘Hans Sama’ Liv’s contract to 2028.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Don’t Fret INTERVIEW: A Horror Game That Should be Music to Your Ears
item.content:encoded:
A horror game where you play as a guitar may sound odd on the surface, and it’s even odder when you actually play it. In my Summer Game Fest 2026 demo, I quickly learned that Don’t Fret is a finely tuned game with scares and notable influences. All of which are discussed in my special interview with the game’s creative director and producer.

Don’t Fret is a play on words as you take control of Fret, a literal guitar trapped in a musical school nightmare. It has that cutesy Five Nights at Freddy’s and Poppy Playtime vibe that makes its actual contents even more unsettling, from faceless enemies to other monstrosities I won’t spoil.

I had the privilege of speaking to Scary Kid Studios owner Russell McKamey (and met co-creator Ryan Giordano) at Summer Game Fest 2026, shortly after playing Don’t Fret, to see how on earth he and the team concocted this symphony of strangeness.

‘Silent Hill and Resident Evil Are Big Inspirations’

My first question had to be answered: ‘ Why a guitar for the main character?’

The response:

‘My first instrument was a guitar, so growing up, I learned to play. It’s a really challenging instrument, like most things from a design standpoint. I think that the guitar lent itself to the character design more than other instruments might. Yeah, you have like a flute or a trumpet or a piano, and I did feel like that guitar is probably the most universal of instruments, where just I don’t know, it’s a cozy instrument, you know, felt like it felt like a younger kid could be a guitar, right?’

This told me everything I needed to know about Don’t Fret and the vision here.

You can’t help but love passion projects, something with emotional and historical attachment. It makes even more sense when I realised that Russell is also the co-owner of the Rockit Music YouTube channel—specializing in nerdcore songs, with an audience of 1.5 million.

Music is a central mechanism and plot device in Don’t Fret, and I learned a little more about how big of an impact it has on the game:

‘A lot of games focus on just jump scares, and the cheap thrill, and we want to focus on storytelling, building up tension, and making these really cinematically cool moments.

It’s not a rhythm horror game, you don’t need to know music, but music is embedded in everything: In every puzzle and in every core aspect of the mechanics of the game, you see music everywhere, and so I think that in that regard it’s one of the most unique things in terms of like a horror game that’s out there.’

From my own experience, one second I was hiding from the clutches of a monster, unable to fight back as I was only armed with a Guitar Tuner that simply distracts rather than devastates, the next I was thrown into a desperate escape sequence with a thumping track that briefly made me think I was in a Blade nightclub.

Russell explained how you upgrade the Guitar Tuner as you progress, but this feels very much like an isolated horror game where fighting back isn’t nearly as effective as hiding and keeping quiet—again, playing into our sound and music themes.

I expected the usual hide-and-seek motif you get with this brand of horror game, like an Outlast, for example, but I was caught off guard by a couple of puzzles that made me think twice. One required me to flick back and forth between several notes to accurately play a certain jingle, all the while being watched by a faceless teacher that still gives me the heebie jeebies even now.

Interestingly, some puzzles will be randomized, some won’t, and this should add to the game’s replayability. The five-person-strong development team has managed to whip up a complete adventure of roughly ‘six to eight hours,’ and there will be some DLC in the future.

If you’re wondering if this is for you, then talk of other famous horror games could lure you in further:

‘Silent Hill and Resident Evil are big inspirations, I think. We went after this to try to have a visual aesthetic that was reminiscent of a Bioshock, but on the surface, it looked kind of like a nice game, but when you dive into it, it’s got a really gnarly story. It starts to get darker and darker as the game goes on, and there are really nasty elements.’

Russell claims it’s an ‘8/10’ on the Scaro-o-meter scale, and I can definitely see that. Don’t Fret is a visually striking and audibly enchanting experience, and you can check out its release date trailer below.

There’s also a free demo on its Steam page.

The Insider Gaming Discord is the perfect place to let me know what you think about Don’t Fret, and if its sound is cool enough for you.

We also have SGF 2026 previews on Turok Origins, Clutch, Blood Message, Valor Mortis, Escape Academy 2, and Saw: Genesis.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

item.title: Resident Evil 0 Remake Was ‘Rebooted Mid-Dev’, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
A recent claim suggests that Resident Evil 0 Remake was rebooted during development, with a new team now handling it.

There have been longstanding rumors about the development of a Resident Evil 0 Remake, alongside discussions about a Code Veronica Remake. While one of these games has been officially revealed and players are eagerly anticipating its release, the development of the 0 Remake appears to have encountered some challenges. Fortunately, it is now in capable hands.

The claims about a mid-development reboot for Resident Evil 0 Remake come from the latest post on X (formerly Twitter) by Dusk Golem. The insider recently replied to a comment about 0 Remake being handled by the Resident Evil 3 Remake team, stating that Resident Evil 0 remake was “rebooted mid-dev”. After the reboot, the remake is being led by ‘Capcom Div 1″.

Jun Takeuchi leads Capcom Division 1, the company’s flagship development team, which handles mainline AAA entries from series such as Resident Evil, Devil May Cry, Dragon’s Dogma, and more. So fans will be happy to know that the main team at Capcom is handling development of this remake.

Resident Evil 0 was originally released in 2002 and received a remastered version in 2016. This game acts as a prequel to the first Resident Evil title, taking players back to 1998 to investigate a series of unusual murders on the outskirts of Raccoon City. Players take on the roles of two characters: rookie cop Rebecca Chambers and death row convict, ex-Navy lieutenant Billy Coen. Together, they uncover the truth behind the events that led to the horrors of the original game set in the Mansion.

In other news, previous rumors suggest that Resident Evil: Veronica arrives in 2027, with 2028 reserved for the Zero Remake. Additionally, Resident Evil Requiem’s story DLC is further away than players thought. What are your thoughts on Resident Evil 0 remake being rebooted mid-dev? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the merit of Fallout New Vegas remake rumors. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fallout New Vegas Remake Rumors ‘Have Merit’, Insider Claims
item.content:encoded:
An insider has revealed that the rumors about Fallout New Vegas Remake are credible.

There was a report earlier this year about the future of XBOX that mentioned that remakes of Fallout 3 and New Vegas were in the works. The words ‘New Vegas 2’ were also recently uttered by a Fallout writer, giving fans more hope for it. It seems that, according to another insider, these claims are real.

Fallout New Vegas Remake Seems to be In Development

The new claim that the Fallout New Vegas Remake is real comes from a reliable insider, NateTheHate. In one X thread (formerly Twitter), the insider was asked whether the rumors about a New Vegas remake were true. To which Nate replied, stating, “The previous rumors have merit.” The insider didn’t provide any additional information, such as release timelines or how they are treating the upcoming title, but this information is enough to give players more hope.

Fallout: New Vegas was released in 2010 and offers an expansive open world set in the Mojave Wasteland, featuring iconic locations such as the Hoover Dam and the Las Vegas Strip. In this game, players interact with a diverse cast of intriguing characters, confront power-hungry factions, and utilize special weapons to defeat mutated enemies. This title serves as a sequel to the critically acclaimed Fallout 3.

In other news, Fallout 76 is finally getting a long-awaited feature with new ‘CAMPanions’ that join you on your adventures in the wasteland. Additionally, Insider Gaming recently sat down with Bethesda Game Studios to talk about Fallout 76 and the future of PvP, and the PAST of Nuclear Winter. What are your thoughts on rumors about Fallout New Vegas Remake having merit? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the XBOX losing ‘hundreds’ on each console sale. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
